title: Clinical Research Coordinators
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate clinical research projects. Direct the activities of workers engaged in clinical research projects to ensure compliance with protocols and overall clinical objectives. May evaluate and analyze clinical data.

tasks.text: Schedule subjects for appointments, procedures, or inpatient stays as required by study protocols.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform specific protocol procedures such as interviewing subjects, taking vital signs, and performing electrocardiograms.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assess eligibility of potential subjects through methods such as screening interviews, reviews of medical records, or discussions with physicians and nurses.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Prepare study-related documentation, such as protocol worksheets, procedural manuals, adverse event reports, institutional review board documents, or progress reports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inform patients or caregivers about study aspects and outcomes to be expected.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Record adverse event and side effect data and confer with investigators regarding the reporting of events to oversight agencies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor study activities to ensure compliance with protocols and with all relevant local, federal, and state regulatory and institutional polices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Oversee subject enrollment to ensure that informed consent is properly obtained and documented.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain required records of study activity including case report forms, drug dispensation records, or regulatory forms.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Identify protocol problems, inform investigators of problems, or assist in problem resolution efforts, such as protocol revisions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review proposed study protocols to evaluate factors such as sample collection processes, data management plans, or potential subject risks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with investigators to prepare presentations or reports of clinical study procedures, results, and conclusions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Track enrollment status of subjects and document dropout information such as dropout causes and subject contact efforts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Code, evaluate, or interpret collected study data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Direct the requisition, collection, labeling, storage, or shipment of specimens.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct research staff in scientific and procedural aspects of studies including standards of care, informed consent procedures, or documentation procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain contact with sponsors to schedule and coordinate site visits or to answer questions about issues such as incomplete data.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare for or participate in quality assurance audits conducted by study sponsors, federal agencies, or specially designated review groups.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Order drugs or devices necessary for study completion.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Contact outside health care providers and communicate with subjects to obtain follow-up information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in the development of study protocols including guidelines for administration or data collection procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with health care professionals to determine the best recruitment practices for studies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate with laboratories or investigators regarding laboratory findings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review scientific literature, participate in continuing education activities, or attend conferences and seminars to maintain current knowledge of clinical studies affairs and issues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Organize space for study equipment and supplies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop advertising and other informational materials to be used in subject recruitment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Dispense medical devices or drugs, and calculate dosages and provide instructions as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Arrange for research study sites and determine staff or equipment availability.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interpret protocols and advise treating physicians on appropriate dosage modifications or treatment calculations based on patient characteristics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Contact industry representatives to ensure equipment and software specifications necessary for successful study completion.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Register protocol patients with appropriate statistical centers as required.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Solicit industry-sponsored trials through contacts and professional organizations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in preparation and management of research budgets and monetary disbursements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
